LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF DEVLIN/MCNALLY CONSTRUCTION INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. TO PREVAIL ON ITS FORECLOSURE OF MECHANIC'S LIEN CLAIM, PLAINTIFF IS OBLIGATED TO SHOW THE REASONABLE VALUE OF ITS WORK/SERVICES, BECAUSE THE AMOUNT DUE IS THE LESSER OF THE REASONABLE VALUE OF THE WORK PROVIDED BY THE CLAIMANT AND THE CONTRACTUALLY AGREED PRICE. (CIV. CODE 8430(A)(1).) "THE ACTUAL AMOUNT DUE ON THE LIEN PRESENTS A QUESTION OF FACT FOR THE TRIAL COURT." (BASIC MODULAR FACILITIES, INC. V. EHSANIPOUR (1999) 70 CAL.APP.4TH 1480, 1485.) IN THIS CASE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN BY UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE THE REASONABLE VALUE OF ITS SERVICES, AND IT IS EVEN UNCLEAR WHAT AMOUNT OF DAMAGES PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING TO RECOVER. PLAINTIFF'S POINTS AND AUTHORITIES APPEAR TO ARGUE THAT $520,270.00 (THE AMOUNT OF THE ORIGINAL RECORDED LIEN) IS OWED. PLAINTIFF'S UMF 9 STATES THAT MISSION HUB LLC FAILED TO PAY "NEARLY $500,000.00" FOR THE SERVICES. PLAINTIFF'S UMF 24 REFERS TO THE $348,849.52 JUDGMENT THAT PLAINTIFF OBTAINED AGAINST MISSION HUB LLC. UMF 29 STATES THAT PLAINTIFF MADE IMPROVEMENTS "RESULTING IN APPROXIMATELY A TWENTY-FIVE PERCENT OR TWO AND A HALF MILLION DOLLAR INCREASE IN THE PROPERTY'S VALUE." THE COURT CANNOT GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BASED UPON SUCH UNCERTAIN AND VARYING APPROXIMATIONS. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion And Devlinmcnally Construction, Inc.S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karissa Fox, Esq. 415-394-6688 of Smith Currie for Plaintiff.
Nick Reckas, Esq. 415-464-0913 for Defendant Drolapas Family Trust, and especially appears for Atty. Gavin.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the reasons for the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's Counsel argues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling in its entirety. Plaintiff Devlin/McNally Construction Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff failed to maintain its initial burden of production.

To prevail on its foreclosure of mechanic's lien claim, Plaintiff is obligated to show the reasonable value of its work/services, because the amount due is the lesser of the reasonable value of the work provided by the claimant and the contractually agreed price. (Civ. Code 8430(a)(1).) "The actual amount due on the lien presents a question of fact for the trial court." (Basic Modular Facilities, Inc. v. Ehsanipour (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 1480, 1485.) In this case, Plaintiff has not shown by undisputed evidence the reasonable value of its services, and it is even unclear what amount of damages Plaintiff is seeking to recover. Plaintiff's points and authorities appear to argue that $520,270.00 (the amount of the original recorded lien) is owed. Plaintiff's UMF 9 states that Mission HUB LLC failed to pay "nearly $500,000.00" for the services. Plaintiff's UMF 24 refers to the $348,849.52 judgment that Plaintiff obtained against Mission HUB LLC. UMF 29 states that Plaintiff made improvements "resulting in approximately a twenty-five percent or two and a half million dollar increase in the property's value." The court cannot grant summary adjudication based upon such uncertain and varying approximations.

Counsel for Defendant is required to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
